text: I start with dividing the inferior pulmonary ligament after retracting the lung anteriorly.
Next, I dissect the anterior hilum to expose the anterior pulmonary vein after retracting the lung poster.
I then dissect the tissue between the fissure to get to the pulmonary artery at the level of the lingular artery takeoff.
I divide the thin attachment between the upper and lower lobes.
After identifying the lingular branch of the pulmonary artery, I start the division of anterior fissure with robot blue load stapler.
Next, I mobilize and divide the lingular branch of superior pulmonary vein.
and then dissect around the lingular bronchus.
I continue the division of the anterior fissure using robot blue load stapler.
I create a tunnel from the anterior portion of the lingular bronchus to the lingular branch of the pulmonary artery to complete the fissure using the robot blue load stapler.
I then divide the lingular branch of the pulmonary artery using robot vascular stapler.
Next, I divide the lingular bronchus with robot glulose stapler from the posterior inferior port.
We inject three cc's of ICG and use the firefly function to identify the border between the perfused and non-perfused parts of the lung.
I use a robot blue-low stapler to divide the parenchyma.
I staple the lung from the central portion of the lung going peripherally in a single staple line.
